summary: Applied ADR 0225 on proxmox_florin by bootstrapping a durable server-resident ansible-pull reconcile loop, minting a least-privilege Gitea read token for the host, correcting repeated host-local replays with a dedicated local ansible-pull inventory, publishing the final verified worktree into the private Gitea source, and proving the timer-backed systemd path end to end through a successful latest-main host-local receipt.

verification.observed: `uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml pytest -q tests/test_server_resident_reconciliation.py` passed with `7 passed in 0.06s`, `ansible-playbook -i inventory/hosts.yml playbooks/server-resident-reconciliation.yml --syntax-check` passed, and `./scripts/validate_repo.sh data-models architecture-fitness generated-docs agent-standards` passed from `/Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/proxmox_florin_server/.worktrees/ws-0225-live-apply` once the ADR index and generated truth surfaces were refreshed.

The isolated worktree replay installed `ansible-core`, the managed askpass helper, wrapper, and `lv3-server-resident-reconciliation.timer` on `proxmox_florin`, mirrored the low-privilege Gitea token to `/etc/lv3/server-resident-reconciliation/gitea-read-token`, and later re-rendered `/var/lib/lv3/server-resident-reconciliation/ansible-pull-inventory.yml` plus the wrapper after the latest-main replay exposed an `ansible-pull` checkout mismatch on repeated runs.

The private repo `ops/proxmox_florin_server` published final synthetic snapshot commit `e24e0cf31d5cf1e7ae9298571f1a196ebdffdbce` for source commit `08ef5c90d8df239c3be00ef6b98a4fbb47b26cb4`, which made the corrected dedicated runtime inventory path available to the host-local `ansible-pull` runner without changing the trusted source URL.

On `2026-03-28T17:26:50Z`, `sudo systemctl start lv3-server-resident-reconciliation.service` completed successfully on `proxmox_florin`, `/srv/proxmox_florin_server` checked out `e24e0cf31d5cf1e7ae9298571f1a196ebdffdbce`, the working tree stayed clean, and `/var/lib/lv3/server-resident-reconciliation/receipts/latest.json` recorded `status: ok`, `exit_code: 0`, `duration_seconds: 10`, and `inventory_path: /var/lib/lv3/server-resident-reconciliation/ansible-pull-inventory.yml`.

notes: Gitea 1.25.5 accepted admin-token auth for user lifecycle calls but required basic auth as the restricted service identity for access-token listing, deletion, and creation, so the bootstrap now resets that restricted password only when minting or rotating the mirrored read token.

The trusted host-local source is the private Gitea repository, not GitHub origin. The final latest-main replay therefore published synthetic snapshot commit `e24e0cf31d5cf1e7ae9298571f1a196ebdffdbce` before the last manual systemd replay.

Repeated host-local replays initially failed because `ansible-pull` reused the repo inventory for its own checkout step after the first run, which left no matching `localhost` or hostname target. The final fix renders `/var/lib/lv3/server-resident-reconciliation/ansible-pull-inventory.yml` so checkout and playbook execution both succeed on repeat runs.

This workstream became the final verified integration step, so the protected release and canonical-truth files were updated on the same branch after the host-local ansible-pull verification completed.
